description: Forge Model Registry — curated set of LoRA-compatible open-weight models.

models.name: TinyLlama 1.1B Chat
models.training_notes: Very lightweight. Ideal for experimentation and quick iterations. Limited context window makes it unsuitable for long-form tasks.

models.name: Qwen 2.5 1.5B Instruct
models.training_notes: Excellent value-for-performance. Good for projects needing long context. Strong multilingual capabilities.

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct
models.training_notes: Best-in-class 3B model. Long context window enables complex instruction-tuning. Requires higher VRAM. Gated model — requires HuggingFace authentication.

models.name: Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3
models.training_notes: Strong general-purpose model. Excellent for instruction tuning. Requires significant VRAM — best suited for RTX 4090 or A100.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B Instruct
models.training_notes: Google's latest open model. Strong performance per parameter. Requires HuggingFace authentication for gated access.

models.name: Phi-4 Mini Instruct
models.training_notes: Excellent reasoning capabilities. Very long context window. MIT license enables commercial use. Best for complex instruction-tuning tasks.

rtx_4090_24gb.name: NVIDIA RTX 4090 24GB
rtx_4090_24gb.notes: Best consumer GPU for fine-tuning. Handles all models in registry at rank 16.

rtx_4080_16gb.name: NVIDIA RTX 4080 16GB
rtx_4080_16gb.notes: Good for models up to 4B parameters. May need gradient accumulation for 7B.

rtx_4060_8gb.name: NVIDIA RTX 4060 8GB
rtx_4060_8gb.notes: Suitable for 1-2B models with low LoRA rank. Use gradient checkpointing.

rtx_3060_12gb.name: NVIDIA RTX 3060 12GB
rtx_3060_12gb.notes: Good budget option. Handles up to 3B models with LoRA rank 8-16.

tesla_t4_16gb.name: NVIDIA Tesla T4 16GB
tesla_t4_16gb.notes: Common cloud GPU. Good for 1-3B models. Limited compute vs RTX cards.

tesla_p100_16gb.name: NVIDIA Tesla P100 16GB
tesla_p100_16gb.notes: Older architecture but sufficient VRAM. FP16 only (no BF16).

rtx_3050_6gb.name: NVIDIA RTX 3050 6GB
rtx_3050_6gb.notes: VRAM-limited. Only smallest models at rank 4-8. Expect OOM on 3B+.

gtx_1650_4gb.name: NVIDIA GTX 1650 4GB
gtx_1650_4gb.notes: Insufficient VRAM for LoRA fine-tuning. Consider cloud GPU options.

a100_40gb.name: NVIDIA A100 40GB
a100_40gb.notes: Datacenter GPU. Handles all models comfortably. Fastest training speeds.

a100_80gb.name: NVIDIA A100 80GB
a100_80gb.notes: Maximum capacity. Handles all models at high batch sizes and ranks.

adamw.name: AdamW
adamw.description: Adam with decoupled weight decay. The standard choice for most fine-tuning tasks.
disadvantages: Higher memory usage (stores two momentum buffers)
adamw.recommended_use: Default for all fine-tuning. Works well across model sizes.

paged_adamw.name: Paged AdamW 8-bit
paged_adamw.description: Memory-efficient AdamW with paged optimization states. Reduces VRAM usage.
paged_adamw.recommended_use: Use when VRAM is limited. Good for 7B models on 16GB GPUs.

adafactor.name: Adafactor
adafactor.description: Adaptive learning rate with reduced memory footprint. No momentum buffers.
adafactor.recommended_use: Memory-constrained scenarios. Fallback when AdamW OOMs.

sgd.name: SGD
sgd.description: Stochastic gradient descent with momentum. Simple and well-understood.
sgd.recommended_use: Rarely used for LoRA. Prefer AdamW unless experimenting.

linear.name: Linear
linear.description: Linearly decays the learning rate from initial to zero over the training steps.
linear.recommendation: Start with linear unless you have a specific reason to change.

cosine.name: Cosine
cosine.description: Cosine annealing schedule. Smooth decay from initial to zero.
cosine.recommendation: Good alternative to linear. Slightly smoother decay curve.

constant.name: Constant
constant.description: Fixed learning rate throughout training. No decay.
constant.recommendation: Not recommended for production. Use only for quick tests.

polynomial.name: Polynomial
polynomial.description: Polynomial decay with configurable power. Flexible schedule shape.
polynomial.recommendation: Advanced option. Use when linear/cosine don't meet your needs.

fp16.name: FP16 (Half Precision)
fp16.description: 16-bit floating point. Supported by most modern GPUs.
fp16.compatibility: Turing architecture and newer (RTX 20xx+, T4, V100, A100)
fp16.notes: May cause overflow in some models. BF16 is generally preferred.

bf16.name: BF16 (Brain Floating Point)
bf16.description: 16-bit with wider exponent range. Same range as FP32, reduced precision.
bf16.notes: Recommended for most training. Better numerical stability than FP16.

fp32.name: FP32 (Full Precision)
fp32.description: 32-bit floating point. Maximum precision, maximum memory usage.
fp32.notes: Not recommended for LoRA. Uses 2x memory with minimal quality gain.
